_comment: Single source of truth for the governed SDK surface allowlist (AC-074 / REQ-053). Both the Python suite (src/python/tests/test_surface.py) and the TypeScript suite (src/ts/tests/surface.test.ts) load THIS file — there is no per-binding duplicate literal, so the two bindings cannot carry divergent copies (cross-binding parity, P2). Each suite asserts its INTROSPECTED live command surface is a SUBSET of `allowlist` (P1, never equality) and that `core` is present and live. As of Slice 30 the four `read.*` members are LIVE: each suite introspects the `read` namespace and asserts the four verbs are present-in-the-live-surface (a removal fails), in addition to the subset check. 0.8.6 Slice 10 (OPP-5): `embed` is a governed read-path primitive (Engine.embed, same name in Py+TS) — HITL-signed 2026-06-26; it was shipped Python-only in 0.8.4 and is now brought to Py↔TS parity. 0.8.19 Slice 10 (OPP-12 record-lifecycle Phase-1): `transition` and `purge` are net-new governed lifecycle verbs (Engine.transition/Engine.purge, same names in Py+TS) — the AC-074 governed-surface delta, HITL-SIGNED 2026-07-10 at the 0.8.19 Slice-40 close (delta also carries the non-command types `LifecycleState`/`IllegalTransitionError`/`NotLifecycleAddressableError`/`IdSpace`/`IdSpaceKind`, `PreparedWrite::Node` +`state`/+`reason`, and the `SearchHit.id` `u64→IdSpace` retype; recovery_denylist UNCHANGED — five names; Phase-2 read-view/validity/projection types excluded). Proposed in the Slice-0 design §10.
